name: Suspicious Activity Followed by Endpoint Administration Task
notes: This rule triggers when normal administration tasks (download a file, update user rights, run as another user, etc) are performed after a suspicious activity is detected on the same machine.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Endpoint Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Endpoint Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Endpoint Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Activity Followed by Endpoint Administration Task on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Endpoint Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Activity Followed by Endpoint Administration task

Event description: Detects a suspicious activity (communication with a potential hostile host, process masquerading, password reconnaissance, etc) is followed by a regular administration task (account management, privilege escalation, etc).
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
